coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Do 10 min easy Z2 warm-up, then 3 × 5 min at Z4 (threshold, ~88–92% max HR) with 2 min Z1 recovery between. Finish with 10 min Z2 cool-down for ~35–40 min total. You're in taper week 20, so this is about retaining leg sharpness and neural readiness without accumulating fatigue—don't exceed threshold; the goal is touch, not grind. Expect to run this on tired legs after travel; if you feel flat, drop to 4 min intervals instead and call it a day.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Get 1.5–2 g carbs per kg bodyweight today (assume ~75 kg = 110–150 g), distributed across three meals plus a post-run carb + protein window within 30 min. For the threshold work, fuel with 300 ml sports drink (6–7% carbs) or a gel + water 30–45 min before. Taper is not license to cut carbs—you're preserving muscle glycogen and keeping energy systems primed for a 50K alpine grind. Skip heavy fats around run time.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Target 8+ hours sleep tonight; taper weeks live and die on sleep debt, and you're juggling travel. Elevate legs for 10 min post-run, take a cold shower (90 sec, 12–15°C if you can manage it), and stay off your feet for 2 hours post-run. Skip any ancillary strength or mobility work today—the nervous system doesn't recover faster with extra work. If jet lag is a factor, get sunlight in the morning and skip caffeine after 12 pm.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 14 weeks out from the Alps. This is the window where race visualization stops being optional—spend 10 min tonight running through the first 10 km of Kranjska Gora (the steep technical descent, altitude shift, sparse aid). Taper anxiety is real for hard athletes; channel it into tactical prep instead. Your fitness is locked in. The remaining 100 days are about arriving sharp, uninjured, and mentally dialed.
